build_check.description: Headless launch exits 0 without fatal errors; this 0/1 gate multiplies the rest.

requirements.description: Pieces move on a grid board following defined movement rules (orthogonal, diagonal, L-shaped, etc.). Clicking a piece highlights valid move tiles. Invalid moves are rejected. 0 if pieces cannot move, if movement rules are absent, or if any tile is always valid. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Each piece has a unique ability on a cooldown timer (charge, shield, teleport, area attack). Using the ability consumes the turn and starts a visible cooldown counter. The ability cannot be used again until the cooldown expires. 0 if no abilities exist, if cooldowns are absent, or if abilities can be spammed every turn. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Terrain tiles on the board have mechanical effects: healing tiles restore HP, damage tiles hurt occupants, and blocking tiles are impassable. At least two terrain types with different effects are present. 0 if the board is uniform with no terrain effects or if terrain is purely cosmetic. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Capturing the enemy king wins the game. Pieces have HP and can take multiple hits before being removed. Combat between adjacent pieces deals damage based on attacker stats. 0 if there is no win condition, if pieces are removed in one hit with no HP system, or if combat has no visible feedback. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

The full loop connects: title -> campaign level select -> battle -> victory/defeat -> next level or retry. Completing levels unlocks new ones. 0 if any transition is missing or if there is no campaign progression. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

At least four distinct piece types exist with different movement patterns, abilities, and stats. Each type has a unique sprite and tactical role. 0 if fewer than three types exist or if types are functionally identical. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

At least three terrain tile types exist with visually distinct appearances and mechanically different effects. Terrain placement varies between levels creating different tactical puzzles. 0 if terrain is uniform or if only one special tile type exists. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

A campaign of at least five levels exists with escalating difficulty: later levels introduce new piece types, more complex terrain, and stronger AI. 0 if there is only one level or if difficulty does not increase. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

New pieces are unlocked through campaign progression, expanding the player's roster for future levels. The unlock is communicated with a reveal moment. 0 if all pieces are available from the start or if no unlock system exists. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The AI opponent uses abilities and terrain strategically, not just random moves. The AI prioritizes healing tiles, uses abilities when advantageous, and protects its king. 0 if the AI moves randomly or never uses abilities. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The HUD shows selected piece info (type, HP, ability, cooldown), turn indicator, and level objective in clear non-overlapping regions. 0 if piece info, turn state, or objectives are missing. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

The board grid is clearly rendered with distinct tile borders. Terrain types are visually distinguishable. Player and enemy pieces are clearly differentiated. Move highlights and ability ranges are shown. 0 if tiles are indistinguishable or if sides cannot be told apart. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

Actions produce visible feedback: moving animates the piece, abilities show effects (projectiles, shields, teleport flash), damage shows HP change, terrain effects trigger visually, and captures show removal effects. 0 if actions produce no observable change. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

Overall art coherence: board, pieces, terrain, UI, and effects belong to a consistent medieval-fantasy visual language with glowing runes and stone textures. 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

Piece sprites are real illustrated or pixel-art fantasy warriors, not colored circles or default shapes. Different types have unique silhouettes and ability indicators. 0 if pieces are plain shapes. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

The board uses real textured stone tiles with terrain overlays (glowing runes, cracks, vegetation). 0 if the board is a plain colored grid. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets with fantasy chrome: ability buttons have illustrated frames, cooldown displays are themed, and menus have authored backgrounds. 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

Animation and atmosphere: piece movement, ability activation, combat hits, terrain effects, and turn transitions use animation or particles. The board has ambient effects (glowing tiles, drifting particles). 0 if all events snap instantly. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.
